channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Britain left the EU 10 years ago. Its politics has been an unruly mess
item.description: Brexit fractured the European Union, and broke British politics. A decade on, millions of voters have deserted the two big parties for alternatives.
item.content:encoded:
Brexit fractured the European Union, and broke British politics. A decade on, millions of voters have deserted the two big parties for alternatives.

(Image credit: Kirsty Wigglesworth)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Gunman kills an officer at a Montreal hotel before being shot dead, police say
item.description: Police say a suspect armed with a long gun opened fire at a Montreal hotel, killing a police officer before officers returned fire, killing him.
item.content:encoded:
Police say a suspect armed with a long gun opened fire at a Montreal hotel, killing a police officer before officers returned fire, killing him.

(Image credit: Christopher Katsarov)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: 2 students in custody after shooting at high school in Philippines kills 3
item.description: An investigation is underway to determine the cause. Police said the suspects claimed they were bullied at school.
item.content:encoded:
An investigation is underway to determine the cause. Police said the suspects claimed they were bullied at school.

(Image credit: James Daantos)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Alan Greenspan, the legendary former Federal Reserve chair, dies
item.description: During his chairmanship, Greenspan was celebrated as possibly the best central banker in history. But later, his reputation was tarnished by the worst financial crisis since the Great Depression.
item.content:encoded:
During his chairmanship, Greenspan was celebrated as possibly the best central banker in history. But later, his reputation was tarnished by the worst financial crisis since the Great Depression.

(Image credit: Paul J. Richards)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: UK PM Keir Starmer resigns. And, US and Iran agree to roadmap for final deal
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation on Monday. And, the U.S. and Iran have agreed to a "roadmap" to reach a final deal within 60 days.
item.content:encoded:
British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation on Monday. And, the U.S. and Iran have agreed to a "roadmap" to reach a final deal within 60 days.

(Image credit: Henry Nicholls)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Keir Starmer has resigned, paving way for a 7th U.K. prime minister in 10 years
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer is stepping down. His likely successor, Andy Burnham, a popular former mayor, would become the U.K.'s seventh prime minister in 10 years.
item.content:encoded:
Prime Minister Keir Starmer is stepping down. His likely successor, Andy Burnham, a popular former mayor, would become the U.K.'s seventh prime minister in 10 years.

(Image credit: Wiktor Szymanowicz)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces resignation
item.description: The U.K. will have its seventh prime minister in 10 years. Keir Starmer is stepping down. His likely successor is the outgoing Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham.
item.content:encoded: The U.K. will have its seventh prime minister in 10 years. Keir Starmer is stepping down. His likely successor is the outgoing Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Ukrainian soldiers march in Kyiv's Pride parade to push for equal rights
item.description: Ukrainian soldiers and their partners march in Kyiv's Pride parade for the right to marry and enter into civil partnerships in a traditional country that's trying to modernize as it aligns with Western Europe.
item.content:encoded: Ukrainian soldiers and their partners march in Kyiv's Pride parade for the right to marry and enter into civil partnerships in a traditional country that's trying to modernize as it aligns with Western Europe.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: Child injuries soar in Israeli air strikes in Lebanon
item.description: The number of children injured by Israeli air strikes have soared since they began in March. One family is dealing with the physical -- and emotional trauma -- of a young girl struck in April.
item.content:encoded: The number of children injured by Israeli air strikes have soared since they began in March. One family is dealing with the physical -- and emotional trauma -- of a young girl struck in April.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Australian police uncover 3 tons of cocaine
item.description: The drug was found on June 19 in plastic tubs buried in bunkers hidden beneath three shipping containers. Two Sydney residents were arrested. They face potential sentences of life in prison.
item.content:encoded:
The drug was found on June 19 in plastic tubs buried in bunkers hidden beneath three shipping containers. Two Sydney residents were arrested. They face potential sentences of life in prison.

(Image credit: Australian Federal Police via AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
